open_obligations.what_is_open: Derive the distributional and inferential theory beyond the established plug-in consistency: an (n h_n)^(1/2) limit law for kernel-localized Dinkelbach endpoint roots in the oracle known-nuisance and feasible estimated-nuisance experiments; an explicit finite-bandwidth bias expansion with primitive negligibility conditions; feasible cross-fitted or debiased linearization of hat_Phi^feas_{sigma,n} including plug-in transform drift; variance and ordinary or directional root-derivative estimation; joint oracle score and multiplier process convergence; uniform active-set denominator slope estimation; and Wald or multiplier confidence sets with pointwise, boundary, tie-law, and uniform coverage for Theta_d(P).
open_obligations.obstruction: The retained positive results identify Theta_d(P) sharply and prove only plug-in consistency of the Dinkelbach roots from deterministic root separation plus a uniform transform LLN. They do not provide the local a_n=(n h_n)^(1/2) stochastic expansion of the random root, the finite-bandwidth bias calculation, the kernel triangular-array CLT, stochastic equicontinuity at random thresholds, variance or derivative estimator consistency, or the directionally differentiable root-map and multiplier approximation needed at Frechet-boundary and tie laws. Adding any of these as an assumption would assume the inferential crux rather than proving it.
open_obligations.attempted: The validated route establishes the identification kernel and the plug-in endpoint consistency route: thm:global-support-function gives the sharp interval Theta_d(P)=[-lambda_{-1}^*(P),lambda_{+1}^*(P)], lem:regular-envelope-derivative gives a well-separated Dinkelbach root, lem:uniform-transform-ulln gives uniform consistency of the known-population-nuisance transform, and prop:regular-dr-endpoint applies the standard separated-zero argument. Earlier attempts to prove Gaussian/Wald and feasible cross-fitted inference required unproved kernel bias-variance, random-root empirical-process, variance/derivative estimation, and feasible nuisance-drift steps; those have been correctly demoted to this OEQ.
open_obligations.partial_result: For each sigma in {-1,+1}, prop:regular-dr-endpoint proves hat_lambda^or_{sigma,n} ->p lambda_sigma^*(P). Hence the ordered plug-in endpoint pair (-hat_lambda^or_{-1,n},hat_lambda^or_{+1,n}) converges in probability to (-lambda_{-1}^*(P),lambda_{+1}^*(P)), the lower and upper endpoints of the sharp identified interval supplied by thm:global-support-function. No Gaussian expansion, feasible Wald inference, variance estimation, derivative estimation, studentization, boundary-directional inference, or uniform coverage conclusion follows from the current positive results.
